message:
fix(btree): split table_seek into read and insert paths; guard cell pointer overlap

table_seek previously left the cursor at EOF when the target rowid fell
off the right edge of a leaf, which is correct for INSERT (we want to
anchor where the new cell belongs) but wrong for SeekGE/SeekLE (we
should advance to the next-larger key via the parent interior node).

Split into table_seek (read path that walks up to the successor) and
table_seek_for_insert (preserves the old anchoring behavior used by
table_insert).

Also adds a guard in read_cell_pointers that rejects pages where the
cell pointer array overlaps the cell content area, returning
DatabaseCorrupt instead of silently reading garbage.

Includes table_seek_bug_test.rs regression test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
